objective: Pilot the always-on governance orchestrator operator experience on a low-risk governance-repo artifact slice, carrying the work from issue-backed plan through packet, validation, alternate review, gate, closeout, PR checks, and merge readiness without expanding autonomous execution authority.
scope_boundary: Use hldpro-governance issue #231 as the low-risk pilot target.
Create issue-backed plan, PDCAR, packet, metrics, cross-review, gate, and closeout artifacts before merge.
Run the packet queue in dry-run/control-plane mode only.
Tie every acceptance criterion to commands, CI, PR state, or tracked artifacts.
Conclude whether the system is ready for broader autonomous execution.
out_of_scope: Executing packet payloads or spawning workers from packet contents.
Changing product repos during the pilot.
Sending packet contents to cloud or Windows model endpoints.
Treating graphify inference as evidence without direct file validation.
research_summary: Issues #223 through #230 are closed and provide the Phase 0 repair, registry, planning gate, read-only observer, local model guardrail lane, packet queue, and self-learning loop. Issue #231 is the final pilot slice. The operator instruction for this run is `proceed, loop through, no HITL`, which serves as the plan-boundary approval for this low-risk governance-repo pilot while preserving strict HALT behavior after the plan boundary.

sprints.name: Pilot Artifacts
sprints.goal: Create the complete issue-backed pilot artifact chain.
tasks: Create the #231 structured plan and PDCAR.
Create a dry-run SoM packet with issue, plan, scope, validation, review, fallback, and known-failure context fields.
Create pilot metrics and gate artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: The pilot creates issue-backed plan artifacts before code or artifact execution.
Packet metadata references the plan, validation commands, review artifacts, and gate evidence.
The packet queue remains dry-run/control-plane only.

sprints.name: Review Gate
sprints.goal: Record independent review, completion gate, and closeout evidence.
tasks: Run alternate-family review and record findings.
Record gate evidence with commands, PR/CI expectations, and residual risks.
Run closeout hook, graph/wiki refresh, and compendium refresh.
acceptance_criteria: Independent review and completion gate pass before closeout.
All acceptance criteria have evidence tied to commands, CI, PRs, or artifacts.
Pilot closeout states readiness for broader autonomous execution.

sprints.name: Registry And Validation
sprints.goal: Document pilot metrics and governance-surface coverage.
tasks: Classify pilot gate and metrics paths as governance surface.
Update registry and data dictionary documentation.
Run local validators and rely on PR checks before merge.
acceptance_criteria: Pilot artifacts cannot change without issue-backed plan coverage.

specialist_reviews.focus: Artifact completeness, dry-run packet queue behavior, gate evidence, validation coverage, and readiness conclusion.
specialist_reviews.summary: The pilot uses tracked governance artifacts and does not execute packet payloads or grant broader authority.

alternate_model_review.summary: Alternate-family review is required for the end-to-end pilot and readiness conclusion. Findings are recorded in the #231 cross-review artifact.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Run #231 as a low-risk governance-repo pilot producing plan, packet, metrics, review, gate, closeout, PR, CI, and merge evidence only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Complete pilot artifacts, validate locally, obtain alternate-family review, close out, open PR, and merge after checks pass.

material_deviation_rules: If the pilot requires executing packet payloads, halt and defer worker execution to a later issue.
If independent review or PR checks fail, do not claim broader readiness.
If any artifact lacks cited command, CI, PR, or file evidence, add evidence before closeout.
If broader autonomous execution appears desirable, record it as a readiness conclusion, not as authority granted by this PR.
approved_by: operator instruction: proceed, loop through, no HITL
